title: 2901 Youth Worker/Student Support Officer-Half day work/School/Hougang
description:
Name of School：Yuying Secondary School
Contract Period: From 2 Mar 2026 to 28 Feb 2027.

Personnel to be deployed at the following hours:

• Monday to Friday: 12 p.m. - 5 p.m.

• For school holidays, personnel will be informed by the Vice-Principal and will be required to report for duty to organise and support holiday programmes.

• Service not required on Saturdays, Sundays and gazetted public holidays.

• The exact working schedule will be confirmed by the school upon award of contract.

• Personnel will be notified by the Vice-Principal if there is a need to report for work for additional ad-hoc sessions, giving at least 48 hours notice.

• Total estimated 875 hours

Job Scopes：
Programme Development and Planning:

● Design and implement a regular after-school engagement programme during term time and school holidays for students who need greater after-school supervision and support (known as the SPACE Crew), utilising the affordances of the SPACE Room to promote a sense of self- and peer-leadership and to foster a sense of school connectedness and belonging. This includes:

○ Co-creating engaging and developmentally-appropriate activities with students that cater to the diverse interests and needs of students.

○ Developing a schedule that balances academic, recreational, and skill-building activities.

○ Maintaining an organised and well-equipped programme venue (SPACE room), ensuring the safety and well-being of all students participating in the programme.

○ Establishing and enforcing behavioural expectations and discipline policies.

○ Working closely with school staff to ensure seamless coordination between the after-school programme.

○ Close communication with key personnel involved in GEAR-UP regarding programmes, student progress, and student concerns.

Mentoring and Guidance:

• Foster a sense of belonging and inclusivity among students by building positive relationships with students and among students. This includes:

o Following up with student absences.

o Regular conversations with students and identify issues affecting them and how their needs can be met.

o Emotional support for students who may need assistance with personal or academic challenges during and after school.

Other duties:

As required by school due to exigencies of service, including monitoring student safety and well-being during school programmes and activities.

Requirements：
Qualifications:

• Degree / Relevant experience in GEAR-UP youth work.

Skill & Experience :

• Possess skills related to programme planning and implementation, rapport building, effective interpersonal interactions and problem-solving.

• Preferably have at least 3 years of experience working in a secondary school environment.

• Prior experience in working with at-risk students and/or students with additional needs.

• Experience in designing, planning and facilitating group guidance programmes for at-risk students and/or students with additional needs during term time and school holidays.

Maxhunt Resource Pte Ltd | EA Licence: 22C1322

•Our company goal is to gain recognition for our staffing solutions, to discover new opportunities for growth, to support customers’ work-life is our value. Aside from business, we also put great effort to contribute for a better society.
